name: Northern Route and Iron Mountains Greenway Loop
description: This moderate 8.4-mile circular jogging route in Portugalete offers estuary views, a section of the Camino del Norte, and cultural landmarks
region_intro: Jogging routes around Portugalete offer diverse terrain along the Bilbao estuary, blending natural beauty with historical urban paths. The region features flat, paved promenades, such as the Paseo de la Canilla, ideal for running, alongside the iconic Bizkaia Bridge. While the old town presents narrow, sometimes steep streets, green spaces like Azeta Park provide pleasant environments for exercise. The landscape is characterized by its waterfront setting, connecting to neighboring towns and offering varied elevations from minimal to moderate climbs. Best jogging routes around Portugalete The most popular jogging route is Tour of Biscay Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes around 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route allows runners to experience the Bizkaia Bridge and Playa de Ereaga on mostly paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Portugalete, Sestao y Barakaldo — circular desde Pedro Ikaza Kalea, a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This route connects multiple towns, offering varied urban and riverside scenery with moderate elevation gain. Local runners also love the Northern Route and Iron Mountains Greenway Loop, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Jogging around Portugalete is defined by scenic estuary promenades, historical urban paths, and green park areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat runs to more challenging routes with moderate climbs. The routes in Portugalete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Portugalete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Portugalete?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Portugalete, offering a variety of experiences for joggers. These include easy, moderate, and more challenging paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Portugalete?
A: Jogging routes in Portugalete feature diverse terrain. You'll find flat, paved promenades along the Bilbao estuary, such as the Paseo de la Canilla, which are ideal for easy runs. Some routes also venture into the historic old town with its narrow, sometimes steep streets, or through green spaces like Azeta Park. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120508276/tour-of-biscay-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer mostly paved surfaces, while others might include varied landscapes with moderate climbs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Portugalete?
A: Yes, Portugalete offers several excellent circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120508276/tour-of-biscay-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.7-mile (7.5 km) loop. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120434697/northern-route-and-iron-mountains-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Northern Route and Iron Mountains Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 8.4 miles (13.5 km) through varied landscapes.

Q: What are the most scenic running routes in Portugalete?
A: For breathtaking views, the promenade along the Bilbao estuary (Paseo de la Canilla) is highly recommended. It offers panoramic vistas of the estuary and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.visitbiscay.eus/en/-/portugalete-a-town-with-views-over-the-estuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaia Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120508276/tour-of-biscay-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay Trail&lt;/a&gt; also allows runners to experience the Bizkaia Bridge and the nearby Playa de Ereaga.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in Portugalete?
A: Yes, the flat, paved sections of the estuary promenade are particularly suitable for families. These routes offer easy running conditions and scenic views without significant elevation changes, making them enjoyable for all ages. Azeta Park also provides a pleasant, green environment for exercise.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Portugalete?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on public paths and promenades in Portugalete, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always a good idea to check local signage for specific restrictions in parks or protected areas to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see while running in Portugalete?
A: Jogging in Portugalete offers views of several significant landmarks and natural features. You'll frequently see the UNESCO World Heritage site, the &lt;a href="https://www.visitbiscay.eus/en/-/portugalete-a-town-with-views-over-the-estuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaia Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the expansive Bilbao Estuary. The old town features historical buildings and the Salazar Tower. For natural attractions, you might encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/577101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Arena Beach&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/569716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barinatxe - The Wild Beach&lt;/a&gt; if your route extends towards the coast.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for a more challenging run?
A: While many routes are relatively flat, some offer more challenging elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120509455/tour-of-biscay-pico-serantes-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay: Pico Serantes Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.9-mile (19.1 km) route with substantial elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a more intense workout and rewarding views from higher points.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Portugalete?
A: The running routes in Portugalete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the scenic estuary promenades, the blend of historical urban paths, and the well-maintained trails that offer options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that connect Portugalete with neighboring towns?
A: Yes, Portugalete is well-connected. The estuary promenade extends to the neighboring town of Santurtzi, allowing for longer, continuous runs. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120556251/portugalete-sestao-y-barakaldo-circular-desde-pedro-ikaza-kalea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portugalete, Sestao y Barakaldo — circular desde Pedro Ikaza Kalea&lt;/a&gt; route connects multiple towns, offering varied urban and riverside scenery over 9.9 miles (16.0 km).

Q: Where can I find green spaces or parks for jogging in Portugalete?
A: Portugalete offers several green spaces suitable for jogging. Azeta Park, spanning over 15,000 square meters, provides a pleasant environment for exercise and connects different parts of the town. Doctor Areilza Park, located near the Iron Pier, is another green area for outdoor enjoyment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Portugalete?
A: Portugalete's mild climate makes it suitable for running year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Summer can be warm, but the estuary breeze often provides relief. Winter runs are also pleasant, though it's advisable to check local weather forecasts.
tips: Site to indicate where to move in Portugalete. Doña María (called La Buena) was proclaimed Lady of Vizcaya by the General Meetings in 1289.
Nice section of the Camino de Santiago del Norte through the Basque Country. The road has paths for bicycles and pedestrians.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Portugalete, San Fuentes — circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: This circular jogging route is approximately 13.5 kilometers (8.4 miles) long. Most people complete it in about 1 hour and 27 minutes when jogging at a moderate pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved, making it a smooth jogging experience. You'll find about 9.3 kilometers of asphalt and nearly 3 kilometers of other paved surfaces. There's also a smaller section of about 330 meters of gravel.

faqs.question: Is the Portugalete, San Fuentes — circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as a 'moderate run' requiring 'good fitness,' the trail is generally considered suitable for all skill levels due to its mostly accessible and well-maintained paths. Its paved nature makes it quite manageable.

faqs.question: What notable sights or features will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque views of the estuary, Santurce, Portugalete, and the 'Abra' (the mouth of the Bilbao Estuary). You'll also pass by the Monument to María Díaz de Haro and a Scenic Section of the Camino del Norte, which is part of the historic Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Portugalete, San Fuentes — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail's starting point is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making access straightforward for those arriving by car.

faqs.question: Does this jogging route connect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates a significant portion of the Northern Route (Basque Country) and also includes sections of the Ezkerraldea -Meatzalde - Enkarterri trail.

faqs.question: Is the Portugalete, San Fuentes — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails with mixed-use paths (pedestrians and bicycles) and open spaces like this one are often dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. The presence of well-kept lawns and large trees along parts of the trail also provides pleasant shaded areas, which can be a relief during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Portugalete, San Fuentes — circular jogging trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments aren't detailed for the immediate trailhead, the trail passes through Portugalete and Santurce, which are urban areas. You'll likely find various cafes and refreshment options within a short distance of the route in these towns.

faqs.question: Are there shaded areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of the trail feature well-kept lawns and large trees, which offer pleasant shaded areas, especially welcome during sunny days.
